_comment: Canonical de-slop ruleset. This file is the single source of truth. The sibling repos (resume/deck builder, the three linters) vendor a synced copy; scripts/sync.py copies this file out and checks for drift. Word and phrase lists merge the workspace canon with the gstack writing rules (github.com/garrytan/gstack, MIT) and the visual-slop tells gstack cites from OpenAI's Designing Delightful Frontends.
generic_copy: take it to the next level

blacklist: Purple/violet/indigo gradient backgrounds or blue-to-purple color schemes
The symmetric 3-column feature grid: icon-in-colored-circle + bold title + 2-line description, repeated 3x
Icons in colored circles as section decoration
Centered everything (text-align: center on all headings, descriptions, cards)
Uniform bubbly border-radius on every element
Decorative blobs, floating circles, wavy SVG dividers
Generic hero copy (Welcome to..., Unlock the power of..., all-in-one solution)
Cookie-cutter section rhythm (hero, 3 features, testimonials, pricing, CTA)
litmus: Brand/product unmistakable in the first screen?
Page understandable by scanning headlines only?
Does motion improve hierarchy, not just decorate?
Would the design feel premium with all decorative shadows removed?
